notes: Confirmed: Both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor have a private canvasSize field but do not expose it as a public CanvasSize property. Every other SkiaSharp view platform (WPF, WinForms, WinUI, GTK3, GTK4, Android, iOS, macOS, Tizen) exposes public SKSize CanvasSize. PR #1832 added the Dpi property to Blazor views but overlooked CanvasSize, creating a parity gap.

reproductionSteps.description: Search Blazor views for canvasSize field and CanvasSize property
Verify other SkiaSharp view platforms expose public CanvasSize property
reproductionSteps.output: Found public SKSize CanvasSize in: WPF/SKElement.cs, WPF/SKGLElement.cs, WinUI/SKXamlCanvas.cs, WinUI/SKSwapChainPanel.cs, WinForms/SKControl.cs, WinForms/SKGLControl.cs, GTK3/SKDrawingArea.cs, GTK4/SKDrawingArea.cs, Android/SKCanvasView.cs, Android/SKGLTextureView.cs, Android/SKGLSurfaceView.cs, iOS/SKCanvasView.cs, iOS/SKGLView.cs, macOS/SKCanvasView.cs, macOS/SKGLView.cs, Tizen/CustomRenderingView.cs, Apple/SKMetalView.cs, Apple/SKCanvasLayer.cs (20+ implementations). Zero matches in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor.

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm Blazor views expose Dpi (from PR #1832) but not CanvasSize
reproductionSteps.output:
SKCanvasView.razor.cs: public float Dpi => dpi; (exposed)
SKGLView.razor.cs: public float Dpi => dpi; (exposed)
No public CanvasSize property in either file.

actionability.reason: Confirmed platform parity gap. The private field canvasSize exists in both Blazor views but has no public accessor, unlike every other SkiaSharp view platform. Trivial one-line fix per view.

actions.description: Apply enhancement and Blazor area labels
Cross-reference issue #1831 and PR #1832 which established the Dpi exposure pattern

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the suggestion! We investigated and can confirm that the `CanvasSize` property is not currently exposed on the Blazor views (`SKCanvasView` and `SKGLView`).

**Investigation:**
Both Blazor view components have a private `canvasSize` field that is used internally for size tracking and rendering, but neither exposes it as a public `CanvasSize` property. Every other SkiaSharp view platform (WPF, WinForms, WinUI, GTK3, GTK4, Android, iOS, macOS, Tizen) exposes `public SKSize CanvasSize`. PR #1832 added the `Dpi` property to the Blazor views but missed `CanvasSize`.

**Workaround:** In the meantime, you can access the canvas size from within the `OnPaintSurface` callback via `e.Info.Size` (which gives the pixel dimensions accounting for DPI scaling).

We've flagged this for consideration. The fix would be a one-line addition to each view:
```csharp
public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize;
```

workarounds: Access canvas size from within the OnPaintSurface callback via e.Info.Size (pixel dimensions with DPI scaling applied)
